Yet, despite the rollback of the last round of anti-inversion regulations in 2017, large US companies have largely stopped leaving the country. title: Stocks Rise, Oil Falls Below $100 on US-Iran Hopes: Markets Wrap
summary: Wall Street is wrapping up a jittery week with gains in stocks and bonds as oil fell on hopes for a diplomatic solution to the Middle East conflict.

title: PE-Backed Robotics Firm Brooks Automation Said to Weigh IPO
summary: Brooks Automation, a robotics and software automation company backed by Thomas H. Lee Partners, is considering an initial public offering, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Trump’s Saudi Deal Spurs Watchdog Interest in Nuclear Settlement
summary: Donald Trump’s plans for a nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia are bringing tensions between US and South Korean reactor developers to the fore, surfacing details of a clash over market access that has garnered interest from regulators.

title: Pork Struggles to Gain Ground Even in Middle of Protein Craze
summary: In a time of belt-tightening and America’s protein obsession, pork should be on a winning streak. It’s cheap compared with beef, which is climbing to record high prices, and it’s the type of clean protein consumers can’t seem to get enough of.

title: Gold Edges Up as Traders Weigh Mideast Conflict, Fed Rate Path
summary: Gold edged higher, holding firmly above the key resistance point of $4,000 an ounce even as the widening conflict in the Middle East fuels expectations for tighter monetary policy.

title: BlackRock Kicks Off $12.3 Billion Bond Sale for Meta Data Center
summary: BlackRock Inc. began marketing $12.3 billion of high-grade bonds to fund a Meta Platforms Inc. data center project as investors grapple with concerns about excessive AI infrastructure spending.

title: Colombia Rations LNG on SPEC Port Shutdown; Business, Schools Urged to Go Remote
summary: Colombia’s government is urging schools and businesses to operate remotely for several days starting next week as the nation braces for natural gas rationing during planned maintenance of its sole import terminal.

title: Modi Seeks to Calm Anger as India Student Protests Widen
summary: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi made a fresh bid to calm mounting anger over examination paper leaks as students continued nationwide protests on Friday, with demands growing for the education minister to step down.

title: Momentum Crash Hits YOLO Traders’ Returns by Most in Four Years
summary: A rapid selloff in the high-flying momentum trade is wrecking the strategy’s staunchest bulls: Retail traders.

title: S&P 500 Steadies as Big Tech Selloff Eases, Oil Dips Below $100
summary: The S&P 500 Index was little changed Friday, steadying from the deepest one-day drop in a month, as Brent crude prices fell below $100 a barrel and the scale of the selloff in Big Tech shares eased up. Major indexes are still headed for weekly losses.

title: Travelers Draws Wave of Bears as Stock Eyes Best Year Since 2000
summary: The best year for Travelers Companies Inc. shares in more than 25 years has unleashed a bevy of downgrades as Wall Street bets that the stock’s gains have peaked.

title: Global Bonds Are Reeling as Oil Surge Rekindles Inflation Threat
summary: Global bonds were pummeled this week by the latest resurgence in energy prices, delivering losses to investors who bet the worst of this year’s rout was over and teeing up credibility tests for central bankers.

title: Gold Miner Cadillac, Backers Raise C$385 Million in Canada IPO
summary: Cadillac Mines Corp. and some of its backers raised C$385 million ($273 million) in an upsized initial public offering, adding to the mining-driven rebound of listing activity in Canada.

title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 24
summary: Brent crude retreated after hitting $100 a barrel amid concerns over the economic impact of high prices and renewed US trade tariffs, yet remained set for a substantial weekly increase as hostilities in the Middle East continue to threaten supplies.

title: Gilts Caught Between Twin Risks From Burnham Debut and Oil Shock
summary: Britain’s bond traders have spent Andy Burnham’s first week in office trying to price two different forces: the spending ambitions of his new government and the impact of oil prices hovering near $100 a barrel.

title: ‘Ridiculously’ Cheap US Bank Capital Risks Trapping Investors
summary: US banks are raising regulatory capital at the tightest valuations since the financial crisis, unsettling the most seasoned investors.

title: Gulf States Set for Debt Binge on Routes to Avoid Hormuz
summary: Traders are preparing for a spike in borrowing from Persian Gulf nations as the region’s oil exporters seek financing to build costly bypasses around the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Profits Are Beating a High Bar and Traders Still Aren’t Enthused
summary: Corporate earnings strong enough to beat the highest expectations in years have failed to impress investors fretting about AI spending and economic growth.

title: Metals Fluctuate as Middle East Escalation Keeps Bulls in Check
summary: Base metals fluctuated at the end of the week as the threat of an escalating conflict in the Middle East — and more turbulence for the global economy — kept a lid on optimism.

title: Oil Keeps Flowing From Red Sea as Hormuz Bypass Stays Open
summary: Millions of barrels of Saudi Arabian crude are still being shipped from the nation’s Red Sea coast to the global market, despite threats and attacks by Yemen’s Houthi militants against the kingdom’s shipping that have the potential to further destabilize the global oil trade.
